title: DenoClaw Agent Configuration
description: Defines an agent's identity, permissions, peers, and execution policy. Lives in data/agents/<id>/agent.json.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this agent does

model.description: LLM model override (e.g. 'ollama/nemotron-3-super'). Inherits from global defaults if omitted.

temperature.description: LLM temperature override. Inherits from global defaults if omitted.

maxTokens.description: Max tokens override. Inherits from global defaults if omitted.

systemPrompt.description: Inline system prompt. Prefer soul.md file instead for longer prompts.

peers.description: Agent IDs this agent can send tasks to (ADR-006). Empty = cannot send to anyone. Closed by default.

acceptFrom.description: Agent IDs this agent accepts tasks from. Use '*' to accept from all. Empty = accepts from no one. Closed by default.

channels.description: Channel names this agent is assigned to (e.g. 'telegram', 'webhook')

channelRouting.description: How messages from assigned channels are routed to this agent

SandboxPermission.description: Deno permission flag for the sandbox subprocess

backend.description: Sandbox backend: 'local' = Deno.Command subprocess, 'cloud' = Deno Sandbox API

allowedPermissions.description: Maximum permissions the agent can use. Actual sandbox permissions = intersection of tool needs and agent allows (ADR-005).

networkAllow.description: Network hostnames/patterns the sandbox can access

maxDurationSec.description: Maximum execution time per tool call in seconds

privilegeElevation.description: Optional broker-owned temporary privilege elevation flow for this agent

enabled.description: Whether broker-side temporary privilege elevation may be requested for this agent

scopes.description: Allowed temporary elevation scopes for this agent

requestTimeoutSec.description: How long an INPUT_REQUIRED privilege-elevation request stays resumable

sessionGrantTtlSec.description: TTL for session-scoped temporary privilege elevation grants

ShellConfig.description: Shell execution mode. 'direct' keeps binary+args semantics. 'system-shell' delegates to a host shell interpreter.

enabled.description: Whether the shell tool may be used when exposed by the runtime

mode.description: 'direct' uses Deno.Command(binary, args); 'system-shell' uses a shell interpreter and enables pipes, redirects and command chaining

warnOnLocalSystemShell.description: Emit a warning when 'system-shell' is used on the local backend

ExecPolicy.description: Shell command execution policy. `security` is the active control surface.

oneOf.description: Allow any direct command without an allowlist (dangerous — does not imply shell interpreter semantics such as pipes or &&)

envFilter.description: Additional env prefixes to strip (on top of LD_*, DYLD_*)

oneOf.description: Allow only direct commands whose binary is in allowedCommands (recommended)

allowedCommands.description: Binary names the agent is allowed to execute (e.g. 'git', 'deno', 'npm')

deniedCommands.description: Keywords blocked anywhere in command string (aggressive match)

envFilter.description: Additional env prefixes to strip (on top of LD_*, DYLD_*)

allowInlineEval.description: Allow -c/-e flags on interpreters (node -e, python -c). Default false = blocked.
